target.name: Sanguinone B
target.motivation: Sanguinones A and B are recently isolated pyrroloiminoquinone alkaloids possessing a rare C4 carboxylic acid. There have been no reports of a total synthesis of the sanguinones. The authors aim to synthesize the full carbon and nitrogen pentacyclic core of sanguinone B, with the correct oxidation at C4.

success_criteria.free_text: Asymmetric synthesis of advanced tetracycles bearing the full carbon and nitrogen framework of sanguinone B, with correct oxidation at C4.

key_disconnections.bond_description: aminal formation and oxidation of methoxy arene
key_disconnections.reasoning: To form the pyrroloiminoquinone from tetracyclic ketone 6.

key_disconnections.bond_description: aza-Michael reaction followed by Friedel-Crafts cyclization
key_disconnections.reasoning: To form the heptasubstituted central aryl ring from tricycle 7.
To access tricycle 7 from arene 8 and alkyne 9, expanding the scope of a previously reported method.

retrosynthetic_strategy.rationale: The retrosynthetic analysis of sanguinone B (2) returns the natural product to tetracyclic ketone 6, with the intention of late-stage aminal formation and oxidation of the methoxy arene to the pyrroloiminoquinone. Tetracyclic ketone 6 could arise from tricycle 7, which was envisioned to undergo an aza-Michael reaction followed by Friedel-Crafts cyclization onto the electron-rich arene to form the heptasubstituted central aryl ring. Critically, tricycle 7 was recognized as a potential product of a Larock/Buchwald-Hartwig annulation cascade, employing arene 8 and alkyne 9 as key building blocks. The C4 acid in the target molecule was reduced to the alcohol to prevent undesired decarboxylation.

thought.background: The synthesis requires two coupling partners for the Larock/Buchwald-Hartwig annulation cascade: known arene 8 and alkyne 9. Arene 8 is prepared using a previously published route.
thought.decision: To prepare alkyne 9, known amino ester 10 will undergo ester reduction to alcohol 11.

outcome.observation: Ester reduction of 10 to alcohol 11 was successful.

evidence.text_span: Alkyne 9 was prepared from known amino ester 10 through a two-step sequence involving ester reduction to alcohol 11 followed by protection as the benzyl ether (Scheme 2A).14 At this stage, we were delighted to find that alkyne 9 is a competent substrate in the Larock/Buchwald−Hartwig annulation cascade, providing a 1:1 mixture of N-deacylated tricycle 12 and acylated tricycle 13 on a multigram scale.
evidence.section: A)Accessing C4 substituted tricycle XX using the Larock/Buchwald-Hartwig annulation cascade

thought.gap: Alcohol 11 needs to be protected as the benzyl ether to form alkyne 9.
thought.decision: To protect alcohol 11, it will be reacted with benzyl bromide (BnBr) in the presence of TBAI and NaH.

outcome.observation: Protection of alcohol 11 as the benzyl ether (alkyne 9) was successful.

evidence.text_span: Alkyne 9 was prepared from known amino ester 10 through a two-step sequence involving ester reduction to alcohol 11 followed by protection as the benzyl ether (Scheme 2A).14 At this stage, we were delighted to find that alkyne 9 is a competent substrate in the Larock/Buchwald−Hartwig annulation cascade, providing a 1:1 mixture of N-deacylated tricycle 12 and acylated tricycle 13 on a multigram scale.
evidence.section: A)Accessing C4 substituted tricycle XX using the Larock/Buchwald-Hartwig annulation cascade

thought.background: The two requisite Larock/Buchwald-Hartwig annulation cascade coupling partners, arene 8 and alkyne 9, are now prepared.
thought.gap: The Larock/Buchwald-Hartwig annulation cascade needs to be performed to access tricycles 12 and 13.
thought.decision: To perform the annulation, alkyne 9 and arene 8 will be reacted under Pd-catalyzed conditions.

outcome.observation: The Larock/Buchwald-Hartwig annulation cascade successfully provided a 1:1 mixture of N-deacylated tricycle 12 and acylated tricycle 13 on a multigram scale.

evidence.text_span: At this stage, we were delighted to find that alkyne 9 is a competent substrate in the Larock/Buchwald−Hartwig annulation cascade, providing a 1:1 mixture of N-deacylated tricycle 12 and acylated tricycle 13 on a multigram scale.
evidence.section: A)Accessing C4 substituted tricycle XX using the Larock/Buchwald-Hartwig annulation cascade

thought.background: Tricycles 12 and 13 are available. The next step is to construct tetracyclic ketone 6. This involves removing protecting groups and performing an aza-Michael reaction.
thought.gap: The aza-Michael reaction needs to be performed on the free secondary aniline.
thought.decision: To achieve this, tricycle 13 will be treated with TFA to remove TES and Boc groups, followed by an aza-Michael reaction.

outcome.observation: Attempts to effect an aza-Michael reaction at this stage resulted in recovery of the starting material.

evidence.text_span: Treatment of tricycle 13 with TFA effected the one-pot removal of the TES and Boc groups to afford the free secondary aniline (not shown). Attempts to effect an aza-Michael reaction at this stage resulted in recovery of the starting material, while alkylation resulted in exclusive acetamide alkylation (see SI).
evidence.section: A)Accessing C4 substituted tricycle XX using the Larock/Buchwald-Hartwig annulation cascade

thought.background: Direct aza-Michael reaction on the free secondary aniline failed, and alkylation led to undesired acetamide alkylation.
thought.gap: A different strategy is needed to incorporate the propargyl group and form the desired ring system.
thought.decision: The crude secondary aniline will be converted to an iminium with propynal 14, reduced with STAB, and desilylated with MeOH/K2CO3 to afford propargylated aniline 15.

outcome.observation: Propargylated aniline 15 was obtained in 83% yield over 2 steps.

evidence.text_span: Ultimately, it was found that the crude secondary aniline could be converted to the iminium with propynal 14, reduced with STAB, and desilylated with MeOH/K2CO3 to afford propargylated aniline 15 in 83% yield over 2 steps.
evidence.section: A)Accessing C4 substituted tricycle XX using the Larock/Buchwald-Hartwig annulation cascade

thought.background: Propargylated aniline 15 is available. The goal is to convert it to tetracyclic ketone 16 via intramolecular Friedel-Crafts cyclization, inspired by reports on terminal alkyne to ketene conversions.
thought.gap: The rhodium-catalyzed terminal alkyne oxidation for intramolecular Friedel-Crafts cyclization is not known for this system, and propargylated amines have not been demonstrated as viable substrates.
thought.decision: Propargylated aniline 15 will be treated with [Rh(COD)Cl]2 in the presence of Yb(OTf)3.

outcome.observation: The desired tetracycle 16 was formed in 24% yield. The reaction was sensitive to indole protecting groups, and yields could not be improved despite extensive experimentation.

evidence.text_span: Ultimately, it was found that treatment of 15 with [Rh-(COD)Cl]2 in the presence of Yb(OTf)316 resulted in the formation of the desired tetracycle 16 in 24% yield.17 This reaction was sensitive to the indole protecting groups, with N-tosylation resulting in trace product formation, while the electron-rich PMB group resulted in increased yields (see SI). Furthermore, despite extensive experimentation, the yields for this reaction could not be improved.
evidence.section: A)Accessing C4 substituted tricycle XX using the Larock/Buchwald-Hartwig annulation cascade

thought.background: Tetracyclic ketone 16 is available, albeit in low yield. The next step is to construct the final tetrahydropyrimidine. This involves protecting the indole nitrogen and then forming the pyrimidine N-oxide.
thought.gap: The oxime formation to introduce the final nitrogen needs to be performed.
thought.decision: Indole nitrogen will be protected with TsCl, followed by attempted oxime formation using NH2OH·HCl.

outcome.observation: Upon attempted oxime formation, pyrimidine N-oxide 17 was serendipitously formed in 75% yield, instead of the expected oxime.

evidence.text_span: Protection of the indole nitrogen with TsCl provided tetracycle 6 in 85% yield, which serendipitously formed pyrimidine N-oxide 17 in 75% yield upon attempted oxime formation to introduce the final nitrogen.18−20
evidence.section: A)Accessing C4 substituted tricycle XX using the Larock/Buchwald-Hartwig annulation cascade

thought.background: The rhodium-catalyzed terminal alkyne oxidation to form tetracyclic ketone 16 resulted in low and variable yields.
thought.gap: An alternative, more efficient route to the tetracyclic core is needed.
thought.decision: Propargylated aniline 15 will be treated with CuTC and TsN3 under conditions reported by Chang to form tetracyclic N-tosyl imine 20 via a Friedel-Crafts cyclization onto an in situ generated ketenimine.

outcome.observation: Tetracyclic N-tosyl imine 20 was formed in excellent yields, providing consistent and scalable access to this intermediate.

evidence.text_span: Ultimately, it was found that treatment of propargylated aniline 15 with CuTC (25 mol %) and TsN3 under conditions reported by Chang 21−23 resulted in formation of tetracyclic N-tosyl imine 20 in excellent yields through a Friedel−Crafts cyclization onto the in situ generated ketenimine (Scheme 2B). This complementary route allowed consistent and scalable access to 20, which could be converted to 16 via imine hydrolysis or converted directly to the pyrimidine N-oxide (vide infra).
evidence.section: B) Formation of N-Tosyl Imine 20 via Cu(I)-Catalyzed Ketenimine Formation

thought.background: A route to the pentacyclic core of the sanguinones is secured. The C4 alcohol oxidation proved challenging in late stages.
thought.gap: The requisite oxidation at C4 needs to be introduced earlier in the synthesis to avoid late-stage redox manipulations.
thought.decision: Alkyne 10 will be transesterified with benzyl alcohol and dibutyltin oxide to provide alkyne 21 protected as the benzyl ester.

outcome.observation: Transesterification of alkyne 10 to benzyl ester 21 was successful.

evidence.text_span: Transesterification of alkyne 10 with benzyl alcohol and dibutyltin oxide provided alkyne 21 protected as the benzyl ester, which readily underwent Larock/Buchwald− Hartwig annulation to form tricycle 22 (Scheme 3).
evidence.section: Scheme 3. Preparation of Pentacyclic Pyrimidine N-Oxide 25

thought.background: Alkyne 21, protected as the benzyl ester, is available. This is the modified alkyne coupling partner for the Larock/Buchwald-Hartwig annulation.
thought.gap: The Larock/Buchwald-Hartwig annulation needs to be performed with alkyne 21 and arene 8 to form tricycle 22.
thought.decision: Alkyne 21 and arene 8 will be reacted under Pd-catalyzed conditions.

evidence.text_span: Transesterification of alkyne 10 with benzyl alcohol and dibutyltin oxide provided alkyne 21 protected as the benzyl ester, which readily underwent Larock/Buchwald− Hartwig annulation to form tricycle 22 (Scheme 3).
evidence.section: Scheme 3. Preparation of Pentacyclic Pyrimidine N-Oxide 25

thought.background: Tricycle 22 is available. Similar to the previous route, the protecting groups need to be removed, and the propargyl group introduced.
thought.gap: The sequence of deprotection, reductive amination with propynal 14, and desilylation needs to be performed to yield alkyne 23.
thought.decision: Tricycle 22 will be treated with TFA, followed by reductive amination with propynal 14 and STAB, and then desilylation with TBAF.

outcome.observation: Alkyne 23 was readily obtained through the sequence of deprotection, reductive amination, and desilylation.

evidence.text_span: As previously described, treatment of 22 with TFA followed by reductive amination/deprotection with propynal 14 afforded alkyne 23.
evidence.section: Scheme 3. Preparation of Pentacyclic Pyrimidine N-Oxide 25

thought.background: Alkyne 23 is available. The copper-catalyzed ketenimine formation proved successful for forming tetracyclic N-tosyl imine 20 in the previous route.
thought.gap: This method needs to be applied to alkyne 23 to form N-tosyl imine 24.
thought.decision: Alkyne 23 will be treated with CuTC and TsN3 to form N-tosyl imine 24.

outcome.observation: Alkyne 23 was readily converted to N-tosyl imine 24 in 65% yield.

evidence.text_span: To our delight, alkyne 23 was readily converted to N-tosyl imine 24 using CuTC (25 mol %) and TsN3 in 65% yield.
evidence.section: Scheme 3. Preparation of Pentacyclic Pyrimidine N-Oxide 25

thought.background: N-tosyl imine 24 is available. The final step is to form the pentacyclic pyrimidine N-oxide.
thought.gap: The conversion of N-tosyl imine 24 to pentacyclic pyrimidine N-oxide 25 needs to be performed.
thought.decision: N-tosyl imine 24 will be treated with NH2OH·HCl.

outcome.observation: Pentacyclic pyrimidine N-oxide 25 was formed successfully.

evidence.text_span: Likewise, treatment of 24 with NH2OH·HCl resulted in the formation of pentacyclic pyrimidine N-oxide 25.
evidence.section: Scheme 3. Preparation of Pentacyclic Pyrimidine N-Oxide 25

thought.background: Pentacyclic pyrimidine N-oxide 25 has been synthesized.
thought.gap: The final step is to reduce 25 to the target molecule, sanguinone B (2).
thought.decision: Attempts will be made to reduce 25 using transition metals or hydride sources.

outcome.observation: Attempts to reduce 25 to 2 using transition metals or hydride sources have thus far resulted in intractable mixtures.

evidence.text_span: Attempts to reduce 25 to 2 using transition metals or hydride sources have thus far resulted in intractable mixtures.
evidence.section: Scheme 3. Preparation of Pentacyclic Pyrimidine N-Oxide 25

Longest Linear Steps.interpretation: The synthetic sequence proceeds in 10 steps from commercial material.

validation.significance: The synthesis provides the full pentacyclic core of sanguinone B, containing the full carbon and nitrogen frameworks, as well as correct oxidation at C4. Key developments include the generation of ketene or ketenimine intermediates for the construction of key tetracycles, which could have wide applicability for the construction of sterically congested benzylic ketones and related carbonyl derivatives.

source.title: Synthesis of the Pentacyclic Core of Sanguinone B Enabled by Alkyne Functionalization.
source.authors: Farhi J, Rezgui SP, Yu H, Stoltz BM.
